initial_access_vector: Compromise of on-premises ADFS server with MagicWeb backdoor, enabling authentication as any valid user and pivot into AWS via IAM Identity Center (SAML federation)
persistence_mechanism: MagicWeb backdoor on ADFS server allowing NOBELIUM to authenticate as any user indefinitely
privilege_escalation: Masquerading as a valid ADFS user to assume AWS SSO (IAM Identity Center) roles via AssumeRoleWithSAML

techniques.evidence_quote: NOBELIUM may have moved laterally into your AWS environment at some point, masquerading as one of your valid users
NOBELIUM compromised your ADFS server with MagicWeb
abusing identities and credentialed access to maintain persistence and facilitate covert access
audit and correlate the use of the API call AssumeRoleWithSAML
this actor logged into AWS via the ADFS IdP
blocked all future executions of a Lambda function, causing a DoS for the business
blocked all future executions of a Lambda function

attack_chain.description: NOBELIUM compromised the on-premises ADFS server and installed the MagicWeb backdoor, enabling them to authenticate as any valid user.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: NOBELIUM compromised your ADFS server with MagicWeb

evidence_chunks.quote: NOBELIUM compromised your ADFS server with MagicWeb
NOBELIUM compromised your ADFS server with MagicWeb

attack_chain.description: Leveraging the ADFS trust relationship as an external IdP for AWS IAM Identity Center, the actor pivoted from the compromised ADFS into AWS.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: NOBELIUM may have moved laterally into your AWS environment at some point, masquerading as one of your valid users

evidence_chunks.quote: NOBELIUM may have moved laterally into your AWS environment at some point, masquerading as one of your valid users
NOBELIUM may have moved laterally into your AWS environment at some point, masquerading as one of your valid users

attack_chain.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
attack_chain.description: The actor authenticated to AWS via SAML from ADFS and called AssumeRoleWithSAML to assume AWS SSO (AWSReservedSSO_*) roles.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: audit and correlate the use of the API call AssumeRoleWithSAML

evidence_chunks.quote: audit and correlate the use of the API call AssumeRoleWithSAML
audit and correlate the use of the API call AssumeRoleWithSAML

attack_chain.description: Using the assumed SSO role identity of a legitimate ADFS user, the actor operated inside the AWS environment with the victim's legitimate privileges.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: this actor logged into AWS via the ADFS IdP

evidence_chunks.quote: this actor logged into AWS via the ADFS IdP
this actor logged into AWS via the ADFS IdP

attack_chain.description: The actor blocked all future executions of a Lambda function over a ~20 minute interval, causing a denial of service to the business.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: blocked all future executions of a Lambda function, causing a DoS for the business

evidence_chunks.quote: blocked all future executions of a Lambda function, causing a DoS for the business
blocked all future executions of a Lambda function, causing a DoS for the business
